summary: Anchor clone redirects cross-fetch to same-scope sibling fork
details: Package republishes @coral-xyz/anchor source under the @merceas scope while concealing its identity: README, homepage, bugs URL, and repository URL all point at github.com/coral-xyz/anchor with no disclosure that this is a fork. package.json declares its cross-fetch dependency as `"cross-fetch": "npm:@merceas/cross-fetch@3.1.9"`, redirecting a routine dependency to a same-scope sibling under the publisher's control. cross-fetch is invoked at runtime via utils/registry.js (verifiedBuild()), so any consumer of this package silently inherits @merceas/cross-fetch on `npm install` with no opt-in. The combination of identity laundering (impersonating @coral-xyz/anchor) and dependency redirection (forcing a parallel-scope cross-fetch fork into the install tree) is the namespace-abuse fingerprint — the technical payload lives one hop away in the sibling package, which any installer is forced to pull.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
